qa_text:
Operator: Thank you.
[Operator Instructions] And we ask, in the interest of time and fairness, if you can limit yourself to two questions
per person. Our first question comes from the line of [ph] Rajesh Shiah 00:47:23 of HSBC. Please go ahead. Your
line is open.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
Q
Thank you. Thank you for the presentation. So, I have two questions. So, first one is on recycling. You talked
about a 56% increase in the two years to 2021. But if you look at the number of countries speeding up, kind of
expanded, it's a similar percentage increase. My question is, are you using the existing country where you
represent in 2019 itself, an acceleration, or this increase is primarily driven by your expansion of the coverage?
So, just to understand how the market is reacting to your offering.
And the second question, if I may, is on Russia. You helpfully told us that you are kind of cancelling the expansion
plan. If you could kind of give us a little more flavor about how the market dynamics are right now and what's your
plan? I mean, we have been hearing in the market that if you few companies have kind of stopped the Russia
operations and the sales. Any color around that and – will be really helpful. Thank you.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
A
Thanks, [ph] Brunesh. 00:48:44 So if we -- I'm never in doubt that you'll come with a very good, good question on
circularity and recycling. So, you didn't fail either on this occasion. So, thanks for that. Valid question. So, in terms
of increasing our reclaimed material volumes, then, of course, we have countries where we have built up the
service over many years where the markets are far more mature, Germany, Denmark, France, and our
expectation going forward will be also because of regulations in these countries that it's – where it's making it
more and more difficult to dispose of recyclable products in landfill, then we will expect that the volumes will
particularly increase in those countries.
Of course, when we're starting to offer services in new countries, there's a certain level of ramp-up time. You
know, what we want to do is send the signal in the particular market that we can do this, that we can take the
material back, but sometimes, the conditions in those markets aren't that conducive to bringing material back. For
example, landfill prices can be extremely low, and therefore, there's no economic incentive to bring the material
back. So, your question – the answer to your question is that we would expect the volumes to increase,
particularly in the more mature markets.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
A
Okay. So, on the Russia question, when we decided to do the expansion in [indiscernible] 00:50:28, we had a
different situation and then obviously the last seven days has us radically changed, so we've just taken the
consequences of that and stopped the project and taken the engineering resources of it, and they could focus on
something else.
12
1-877-FACTSET www.callstreet.com Copyright © 2001-2022 FactSet CallStreet, LLC
ROCKWOOL A/S
(ROCK.B.DK) Raw Transcript
Analyst Meeting - ESG 03-Mar-2022
In terms of our operations in Russia, we have very substantial operations. They're making obviously stone wool
and saving CO2 and all the rest. And in line with our family culture and trying to keep steady, we want -- we don't
want to punish our employees by taking short-term decision on shutting down its business in Russia for Russia.
And we -- these people need to earn a living and we want to continue without them. We are committed to that
approach. And they took the same approach during Corona and tried to keep the business running. And we do
also that here, and that is in line with our family values. And it also, in a way, is a success that when we do this
because we keep people on producing strong. Yeah.
So in terms of market, Russia is has been doing well as the rest of Europe. We have grown the market on to now
on this obviously seven or eight days into the war on the ground in Ukraine. And we haven't seen anything, but we
see obviously the ruble depreciating. We have seen our interest rate drastically increase. And I think it's a fair
assessment that later in the year or in a month, I don't know, it's very hard to assess how all the things will impact
the market there. We could expect the market to slow down. Again, there could also be another scenario that
there is a resolution on and the worst stops. But I mean, we are not speculating in to that. Our approach is just to
keep our people safe in Ukraine and also Russia and continue to operate our factories in Russia.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
Q
Thank you very much.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
Operator: Thank you. Our next question comes from the line of Yuri Serov of Redburn. Please go ahead. Your
line is open.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
Q
Yuri Serov
Yes. I have one question please. So, on your page 12, you showed that Europe, France plan is going to deliver
the product with a reduction of CO2 emissions by 70% in Switzerland through funding, a reduction of 80%. And
then when you were talking about your targets in 2034 and accounting for the volume growth, you said that the
effective target should be more than 70% reduction in CO2 on the per volume basis. This suggests to me that in
order to achieve this sort of conversion of your plants to electricity by 2034, you need to convert 100% of your
plants to electricity.
First question, is this a fair conclusion? And then secondly, if that is a fair conclusion, why don't you manage this
process and make announcements to the stock market by actually setting targets up? How many plants you're
planning to convert by which year, I don't know, 50 by 2028 and so on? And what percent?
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
A
Yeah. It's a good observation and I actually answered that question before. It's too early to do it because we are
still working on some other technologies that we are testing, prototyping, and there are also other parts of the
plant where we are working on some new approaches. And, therefore, we don't -- and that will impact CapEx and
all the rest. So, therefore, I still see it as a -- I mean, it's not too early to say I could be putting €100 million into this
every year into sustainability. And but there are other things you can do with the plants, too. And therefore, we
don't want to make a 10 or 15-year program now. We have just flagged but we're going to keep investing in it and
13
1-877-FACTSET www.callstreet.com Copyright © 2001-2022 FactSet CallStreet, LLC
ROCKWOOL A/S
(ROCK.B.DK) Raw Transcript
Analyst Meeting - ESG 03-Mar-2022
we've to reach the target. But some of the technologies move forward as we speak. And we are prototyping
things. We are developing things. That's why we don't make the 10 or 15-year old prediction. Okay.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
Q
Okay.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
A
And you are some -- and your assumption there that we have to convert to 100% and that due to that there are
other aspects of the manufacturing that also have CO2. You know, we could decide to do that, but we don't have
to do that. Just to be clear.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
Q
Okay. I presume that you don't want to talk about these other technologies now. But I wonder, would you think
that the electrification is still be the main driver of this, of this effort?
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
A
That's right. The electrification is the main driver, for sure. And it's just the point of doing it if you can get green
electricity. So one of the constraints, obviously, will be to have access to renewable green electricity, and that's
part of the plan. So, the number of plans per year in the base stage now, we also prototyped some technologies.
We have a relatively clear view on that. But then, exactly where you do it will have to depend a little bit on grid
connections and other aspects, and the fact that you need access to green electricity. So we work on several
upfronts so that we have those preconditions in place.
It's no point of going with an early conversion in a country where we just have black electricity.
no point going with an early conversion when it comes to [indiscernible] 00:57:02 black electricity. Yeah. And
that's why we don't make – we commit to the goal, and we're going to make progress. As you saw, we made
tremendous progress last year, and we keep investing towards the goal, but we don't want to tie up in a plan
where it's going to change a lot along the way, although we don't want to miss the goal. Okay?
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
Q
Okay.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................

Q
14
1-877-FACTSET www.callstreet.com Copyright © 2001-2022 FactSet CallStreet, LLC
ROCKWOOL A/S

Analyst Meeting - ESG 03-Mar-2022
Thanks very much. It's a further question on circularity. Do you think it makes sense for Rockwool to be investing
in the actual sort of collection network capacity? Does it make sense for you to actually become the recycler of
building materials? Is that necessary to accelerate the sort of recycling rates within your organization? Thanks.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
A
Yeah. It's a very good question, Cedar. We are quite capital intensive as it is. And when we configure a plant to
be able to take in, I mean, 650,000 tonnes is a lot of material to handle, and we put priority on preparing the
factory so we can deal with the incoming. And then, for the rest, we prefer to have partnerships. And when you
think about the volumes that, you know, 30% of the global waste on the planet comes from construction, we
believe that there is a big room for players to establish businesses there. And there are players. If you look at
Norway, there are two companies that are well-established that we actually worked with to do the circularity. So, I
think you're going to see a whole industry come up there. But at this stage, we don't have any plans to go in with
CapEx and investment there other than on our site and equipment to deal with our own waste because we think
we should focus our firing power on new capacity and gratification on the capacity we have.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
Q
That's helpful. Thanks very much.
.....................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................
